content.boundary: Applies to clusters of 2-5 villages under 2,000 inhabitants within reasonable travel distance of each other where individual schools face borderline (not zero-enrollment) viability; excludes single isolated villages with no nearby peer settlement to network with.
content.change_lever: Create formal multi-village school networks (shared staffing pools, joint pedagogical planning, rotating specialist teachers) and permit repurposing of school buildings as multi-service community hubs (education plus childcare/kindergarten/social/cultural services), reducing the isolation of any single small school without full consolidation.
content.name: Networked retention: multi-village pedagogical clusters and multi-service community hubs
content.system_problem: Individual village schools below viable enrollment thresholds cannot independently sustain quality staffing or meet national class-size minimums, but full centralization severs community infrastructure; there is no existing formal Hungarian mechanism analogous to France's RPI clustering or Poland's community-run school model to bridge this gap.
